req.title: Card freeze / unfreeze (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall freeze or unfreeze their card instantly; frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers card freeze/unfreeze with instant state changes, rejection of authorization on frozen cards, boundary thresholds, and a 404 error path. Five tests are designed: (1) EP positive — freeze an active card and verify state + network notification; (2) STC positive — full freeze→unfreeze cycle verifying state transitions; (3) NEG negative — attempt freeze on a non-existent card expecting 404; (4) DT negative — attempt authorization on a frozen card expecting rejection; (5) BVA boundary — toggle freeze at exact min/max card-id boundary values. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down cards, ensuring idempotency.

tests.name: test_freeze_active_card_returns_frozen_state
actions: POST /cards/{id}/freeze with valid card_id owned by caller
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Authorization network notified (notification flag present in response)
trace: R-FIN-035: card freeze toggles state instantly
R-FIN-035: authorization network notified on freeze
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_freeze_active_card_returns_frozen_state(api_client, active_card):
    card_id = active_card["id"]
    resp = api_client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "frozen"
    assert body.get("network_notified") is True

tests.name: test_freeze_then_unfreeze_card_state_transitions
actions: POST /cards/{id}/freeze — move card to FROZEN state
POST /cards/{id}/unfreeze — move card back to ACTIVE state
expected: First POST returns HTTP 200 with status='frozen'
Second POST returns HTTP 200 with status='active'
Both transitions notify the authorization network
trace: R-FIN-035: freeze and unfreeze are both supported toggle operations
R-FIN-035: card state updated instantly on each toggle
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_freeze_then_unfreeze_card_state_transitions(api_client, active_card):
    card_id = active_card["id"]
    freeze_resp = api_client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze")
    assert freeze_resp.status_code == 200
    assert freeze_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen"

    unfreeze_resp = api_client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/unfreeze")
    assert unfreeze_resp.status_code == 200
    body = unfreeze_resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "active"
    assert body.get("network_notified") is True

tests.name: test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404
preconditions: Card ID used does not exist in the system
actions: POST /cards/{id}/freeze using a card_id that does not belong to any account
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing the card not found
trace: R-FIN-035: error pathway — 404 when card does not exist
tests.executable:
import pytest

NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"

def test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404(api_client):
    resp = api_client.post(f"/cards/{NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID}/freeze")
    assert resp.status_code == 404
    body = resp.json()
    assert "not found" in body.get("error", "").lower() or \
           "not found" in body.get("message", "").lower()

tests.name: test_authorization_rejected_on_frozen_card
preconditions: Customer owns a card that is in FROZEN state
actions: POST /cards/{id}/freeze to ensure card is frozen
POST /authorizations with frozen card_id and a valid transaction amount
expected: Response body contains decline_reason indicating card is frozen
trace: R-FIN-035: frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts
tests.executable:
import pytest

DECLINED_STATUSES = {402, 403}

def test_authorization_rejected_on_frozen_card(api_client, active_card):
    card_id = active_card["id"]
    freeze_resp = api_client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze")
    assert freeze_resp.status_code == 200

    auth_payload = {"card_id": card_id, "amount": 10.00, "currency": "USD"}
    auth_resp = api_client.post("/authorizations", json=auth_payload)
    assert auth_resp.status_code in DECLINED_STATUSES
    body = auth_resp.json()
    reason = (body.get("decline_reason") or body.get("error") or "").lower()
    assert "frozen" in reason

tests.name: test_freeze_at_min_and_max_card_id_boundaries
preconditions: System supports card IDs at minimum boundary value (length=1 or lowest valid ID)
System supports card IDs at maximum boundary value (max length / highest valid numeric ID)
actions: POST /cards/{min_id}/freeze using the minimum valid card ID owned by caller
POST /cards/{max_id}/freeze using the maximum valid card ID owned by caller
expected: No off-by-one or overflow errors in card lookup
trace: R-FIN-035: boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested
